drawer-nested.name: Nested

stories.name: Deep Nesting (3 levels)
stories.snippet:
const ThreeLevels = () => (
Open Level 1
Level 1 Deepest nesting: this will scale down twice (scale = 0.90) when all children are open. Open Level 2
Level 2 This will scale down once (scale = 0.95) when the grandchild opens. Open Level 3
Level 3 The deepest drawer. Close this to see the parents scale back up one by one. Close Level 3
Close Level 2
Close Level 1
);

stories.name: Without NestingDrawerProvider
stories.snippet:
const WithoutProvider = () => (

No NestingDrawerProvider — nesting animation is disabled. Drawers work independently.

Open Parent Drawer
Parent Drawer No scale animation will occur when the child opens. Open Child Drawer
Child Drawer Parent should NOT scale — no registry is present. Close Child
Close Parent
);

stories.name: Both Initially Open (defaultOpen)
stories.snippet:
const InitiallyOpen = () => (
Open Parent Drawer
Parent Drawer Both parent and child are initially open. The parent should already be scaled down on mount — no flash.

Note: After closing all drawers and reopening the parent, the child remounts with defaultOpen=true again. Use controlled `open` prop to preserve closed state across parent reopen cycles. Open Child Drawer
Child Drawer Close this to see the parent animate back to full scale. Close Child
Close Parent
);

stories.name: Close Parent from Child
stories.snippet:
const CloseParentFromChild = () => { const [parentOpen, setParentOpen] = useState(false) const [childOpen, setChildOpen] = useState(false) return (

Parent: {parentOpen ? 'Open' : 'Closed'} | Child:{' '} {childOpen ? 'Open' : 'Closed'}

Parent Drawer Open the child drawer, then close the parent from within the child. Both drawers should animate their close transitions.
Child Drawer (Form) Simulates a form completion flow. Clicking "Complete & Close Parent" closes the parent drawer, which propagates close to this child drawer as well.
) };

stories.name: Sibling Drawers
stories.snippet:
const Siblings = () => (
Open Parent Drawer
Parent Drawer Two sibling child drawers share the same parent. Close one and open the other — the parent scale animation should transition correctly between siblings.
Open Child A
Child A Close this drawer, then open Child B from the parent. Close Child A
Open Child B
Child B Close this drawer, then open Child A from the parent. Close Child B
Close Parent
);

drawer-nested.import: import { Description, Drawer, DummyHandle, NestingDrawerProvider, Overlay, Title } from "./drawer";

example: ```tsx import { NestingDrawerProvider } from '@hikidas/react' function App() { return ( <NestingDrawerProvider> <Drawer.Root>...</Drawer.Root> <Drawer.Root> <Drawer.Root>...</Drawer.Root> // nested </Drawer.Root> </NestingDrawerProvider> ) } ```

drawer-nested.description: Provides nesting support for descendant Drawer components. Wrap this around your app (or a subtree) to enable nested drawer features: drag-time ancestor scale interpolation, and cascade close. When no NestingDrawerProvider is present, drawers function independently — the nesting integration is fully opt-in and tree-shakeable.

reactDocgen.description:
Provides nesting support for descendant Drawer components.
Wrap this around your app (or a subtree) to enable nested drawer features:
drag-time ancestor scale interpolation, and cascade close.

When no NestingDrawerProvider is present, drawers function independently —
the nesting integration is fully opt-in and tree-shakeable.

@example
```tsx
import { NestingDrawerProvider } from '@hikidas/react'

function App() {
  return (
    <NestingDrawerProvider>
      <Drawer.Root>...</Drawer.Root>
      <Drawer.Root>
        <Drawer.Root>...</Drawer.Root> // nested
      </Drawer.Root>
    </NestingDrawerProvider>
  )
}
```

stories.name: Snap Points
stories.snippet:
const SnapPoints = () => { const snapPoints = [0.2, 0.5, 1.0] return (
Open Drawer
Three Snap Points Snap points at 25 %, 50 %, and 100 %. Drag slowly to snap to the nearest point, or flick quickly to jump further. Close
) };

Direction Up
Direction Left
Direction Right
Disable Drag Dismiss
stories.snippet:
const DisableDragDismiss = () => { return (
Open Drawer
Persistent Drawer Swiping shows a rubber-band effect but will not close the drawer. Use the close button, ESC, or click outside to dismiss. Close
) };

stories.name: Scrollable Content
stories.snippet:
const ScrollableContent = () => { return (
Open Drawer
Scrollable Content When scrolled to the top, dragging down dismisses the drawer. When scrolled past the top, scroll takes priority over drag.
{Array.from({ length: 30 }, (_, i) => (

Item {i + 1}

Scroll to see more items. Try dragging from the top vs after scrolling.

))}
) };

stories.name: No Drag Zone
stories.snippet:
const NoDragZone = () => { return (
Open Drawer
No-Drag Zones The square below has{' '} data-drawer-no-drag {' '} attribute which disables drag interactions, allowing you to interact with it without accidentally dragging the drawer. This is useful for interactive elements like maps, sliders, etc.
Close
) };
